decision_tree.description: Pick the right deployment strategy for your service

options.label: High traffic, can spare 1-5% for canary

answer.pick: Canary (5% traffic) + automated promotion on metrics
answer.rationale: Spinnaker, Argo Rollouts, or Flagger. Watch error rate, latency, business metrics. Auto-rollback on SLO violation.

options.label: Low traffic, canary won't get meaningful data

answer.rationale: Deploy code, but only enable for internal users / specific cohort. Real production traffic, controlled exposure. LaunchDarkly, Unleash, or Flagsmith.
Two identical environments. Switch load balancer. Instant rollback by switching back. Databases are the hard part — use expand-contract migrations.

answer.pick: Blue/Green via load balancer + DNS switch
answer.rationale: Two identical stacks. Switch DNS or LB. Need careful schema migration discipline. Document the rollback procedure.
Kubernetes rolling update. Set maxSurge and maxUnavailable. Health checks gate the next instance. If health check fails, the rollout stops. Built-in to K8s.
For 1-3 instances, rolling doesn't help. Just take the brief downtime, or do a manual blue/green with one new + one old. Schedule a maintenance window.
